subject: Sports
title: Oxford vs Cambridge: The Boat Race
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Oxford vs Cambridge: The Boat Race.

hook.text: Two elite universities race rowing boats on the Thames. It's been happening since 1829 and millions still watch.

buildup.text: The Boat Race is a 4.2-mile course on the Thames between Putney and Mortlake in London.

discovery.text: Cambridge leads overall wins, but the margins are often agonizingly close—sometimes less than a boat length.

twist.text: In 2012, a protester swam into the path of the boats, forcing a restart. The disruption nearly caused a collision.

climax.text: The race attracts 250,000 spectators along the Thames and millions of TV viewers worldwide despite being amateur.

punchline.text: Two schools, one river, almost 200 years of bragging rights.

quiz.question: How long has the Oxford vs Cambridge Boat Race been running?
